# Perceptual memory — can the dock name & recognize places, things, faces (and be corrected)?

## Provenance

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Research date | 2026-07-08 |
| Subject | Feasibility of persistent, human-correctable visual entity memory (faces / places / things) via image embeddings + a small VLM, for orbit-dock |
| Trigger | "The VLM doesn't remember or correlate — can it name a place/face/thing, recognize it again, and be corrected?" (chat, 2026-07-08) |
| orbit commit at analysis | `3f4b614` (main) |
| Analyst | Claude (Opus 4.8) + 10 web-research sub-agents |
| Method | Literature review only — WebSearch/WebFetch across VPR, few-shot recognition, open-vocab detection, VLM temporal/memory evals, semantic-SLAM, memory-system failure modes. Cited benchmark numbers. **No POC run yet.** Existing orbit face-api stack read from source. |

## Method & honesty

This is a **literature review, not an experiment.** No embeddings were computed on orbit's
own frames; no model was timed on the station's Metal GPU. Every accuracy number below is
from a published benchmark on *someone else's* data, and in-home performance is
consistently *worse* than benchmark — so treat all numbers as **ceilings**. The orbit-side
claims (what face-api does today) are read from `modules/perception/face/` source at
`3f4b614`. The two POCs in §5 are exactly what would convert this review into fact.

## Things to note / takeaways

1. **The architecture (stateless small VLM for per-frame extraction + correlation done in a
   TEXT layer + embedding memory for recognition) is CORRECT — and half of it is already
   built.** confidence: high. **already-have (text half) / steal (visual half).** The
   coherence layer (`perception/grounding.ts`, `memory/longterm/`, committed `84fdb04`) IS
   the text-correlation memory the evidence says to use. Small VLMs measurably fail at
   cross-time correlation, so keeping that in text was the right call.
   Cite: ConceptGraphs/ReMEmbR (established pattern); Qwen2.5-VL temporal evals (§Supporting B).

2. **Do NOT expect the small VLM to correlate across time / images itself.** confidence:
   high. **skip.** Qwen2.5-VL-3B (4-bit) breaks on temporal grounding, multi-image
   consistency (accuracy craters past ~5 images), and in-prompt visual priors ("multi-image
   ICL underperforms no-image ICL"). Bears on: any future "feed it the last N frames" idea.
   Cite: TOC-Bench 7B=27%/72B=34%; "More Images More Problems"; VL-ICL Bench.

3. **Faces (already built) is the strong case; keep it.** confidence: high. **already-have.**
   128-d face-api descriptor + gallery NN (`face/gallery.ts`, threshold 0.6). Near-frontal
   ~99% on benchmark but −10–17% cross-pose/age, and one global threshold is demographically
   unfair (10–100× FMR variance, NIST FRVT). Cite: `face/recognizer.ts:53`, `gallery.ts`.

4. **"Remove face-api" is only PARTIAL.** confidence: high. **watch.** A generic embedding
   replaces face-api's *recognizer+gallery* (identity) only. Face-api also does the **box**
   (faceFollow tracking), **active-speaker** (mouth landmarks → summarizer diarization), and
   **emotion** — none of which a place/object embedding provides. So: fold the face *gallery*
   into a unified store, but a face *detector* stays. Cite: `face/recognizer.ts:25-27,76`.

5. **Distinct whole VIEWS ("the window", "the bookshelf") are viable; within-room CORNERS
   are not (off-the-shelf).** confidence: high. **steal (views) / skip (corners).** Whole-room
   / distinct-view recognition ~65–77% zero-shot (AnyLoc indoor). But distinguishing corners
   *within one room* is perceptual aliasing: indoor within-building VPR is **27–36% R@1**
   (NYC-Indoor; DINOv2 27.6%), and separating "spatially close, visually similar spaces"
   needed triplet-loss CLIP fine-tuning. Our pan/tilt + day/night maximizes the two worst VPR
   failure modes. Cite: NYC-Indoor-VPR; AnyLoc; fine-grained-VPS.

6. **Correction generalizing across viewpoints is the LOAD-BEARING RISK, and the evidence is
   discouraging.** confidence: high. **watch / gate hard.** CLIP/DINOv2 cluster by *camera
   angle*, not identity: same object zero-shot accuracy **collapses 68.7% → 3.0%** on an
   angle change; 67% of images have a NN ≥90° away in pose; off-the-shelf inter/intra-class
   ratio ~1.2. So a one-shot "that's the cat tree" correction fixes near-identical frames and
   does **not** reliably transfer to other angles. Multi-view exemplars + rehearsal are
   required to generalize, and aggressive relabeling corrupts neighbors (backward-transfer
   −18.5%). Cite: ABO viewpoint collapse (2412.19920); Pix3D pose-NN (2311.17938); ObjectPI.

7. **Auto-naming is UNSAFE — human correction is mandatory, not a nicety.** confidence: high.
   **steal (the correction loop) / gate (no silent writes).** CLIP is miscalibrated and
   confidently wrong (emits a wrong name at the same high confidence as a right one), and is
   weak on fine-grained/cluttered/specialized objects (the exact home case). So the system
   must propose tentatively, default low-confidence, and treat the human's correction as
   truth — which ratifies the original design instinct. Cite: 2303.12748, 2402.07410; CLIP
   paper §3.1.4.

8. **THINGS (cropped low-res objects): method exists, reliability marginal, compute unproven.**
   confidence: medium. **watch.** OWL-ViT does detect-and-embed + one-shot image-conditioned
   re-ID natively (+72% one-shot AP50) — the right mechanism. But real accuracy compounds
   down to ~40–60% (low-res −16pp, clutter −7pp, viewpoint −40pp, 1-shot −12pp), and there
   are **no CPU/Metal timing numbers** for OWL-ViT — Metal feasibility "looks poor without
   quantization". Cite: OWLv2 34.9 AP_rare; LR0.FM; ObjectNet; Backgrounds Challenge.

9. **"Store everything" is a NET NEGATIVE; a forgetting policy is mandatory.** confidence:
   high. **steal (the discipline).** Add-all memory measured *worse* than selective (13% vs
   39%). And SOTA memory systems resolve correction/conflict at only 12–50% and often can't
   detect a contradiction — "recognizing a memory is stale ≠ applying the new value". The
   existing curator's confidence + reconcile + decay is the right shape; extend it, don't
   bolt on a naive vector dump. Cite: forgetting-problem study; MemConflict.

10. **A stationary single camera is EASIER for recognition, IMPOSSIBLE for metric mapping.**
    confidence: high. **already-suited.** No parallax → no metric SLAM, no 3D scene graph,
    no "object 1.2m to the left of X". But no trajectory → no drift, no loop-closure, and the
    fixed viewpoint minimizes VPR's biggest failure driver. The dock can build an
    *appearance/semantic* memory, not a *metric* one — which is exactly what we want.
    Cite: monocular scale-ambiguity literature; semantic-SLAM surveys.

## Supporting analysis

### A. Place recognition (VPR)
- Outdoor/distinct zero-shot: AnyLoc (DINOv2+VLAD) 92% urban, **77% indoor avg**; SALAD 91.7%
  Pitts30k. Within-building indoor: NYC-Indoor **27–36% R@1**, DINOv2 27.6%. Viewpoint 90°/180°
  and directional light are the documented worst cases (VPR-Bench, AnyLoc). 320×240 is *fine*
  for DINOv2 (multi-res trained). Compute: DINOv2 ViT-S ~10–35ms CPU, faster on Metal → 4Hz+ ok.
  Sources: AnyLoc 2308.00688, NYC-Indoor 2404.00504, VPR-Bench 2005.08135, SALAD 2311.15937.

### B. Small-VLM ceiling
- TOC-Bench temporal object consistency: Qwen2.5-VL-7B 27%, 72B 34%, humans 89%. TempCompass
  3B=64.4/7B=71.7. Multi-image: single ~40% → <15% at 6+ images. 4-bit costs another ~3–4%,
  hits small models hardest; reasoning degrades more than perception below 3B.
  Sources: TOC-Bench 2605.09904, "More Images More Problems", VLMQ 2508.03351.

### C. Few-shot object recognition + correction
- 5-way frozen-embedding NN: 1-shot ~62%, 3-shot ~72%, 5-shot ~80% (clean 224px, closed set).
  Degrades: −16pp low-res, −7pp clutter, −40pp viewpoint (ObjectNet), −12pp 1-shot-vs-5.
  Correction: label propagation adds only ~7pp at 1-shot (shrinks fast); embeddings cluster
  by pose (viewpoint collapse 68.7%→3.0%); online relabel corrupts neighbors (BWT −18.5%).
  Sources: SimpleShot 1911.04623, LR0.FM 2502.03950, ABO 2412.19920, TPN 1805.10002.

### D. Architecture & memory systems
- Established open-vocab-map lineage: ConceptFusion → ConceptGraphs (embedding + caption per
  node — closest to this idea) → HOV-SG (~55% real-robot success) → ReMEmbR (vector-DB +
  captioner). Human correction is NOT in these — it lives in iTeach/interactive-teaching and
  in *products* (Astro Visual ID, Roomba room labels). Rolling-summary-in-context beats a
  store by ~6pts accuracy but at ~14× tokens / ~10× latency and is lossy + "lost in the middle".
  Sources: ConceptGraphs 2309.16650, HOV-SG 2403.17846, ReMEmbR 2409.13682, LOCOMO/Mem0.

## The two gating POCs (do these BEFORE building anything)

Every research thread independently converged on the same cheap disproof. Both use data
orbit already has (ring keyframes / Takes) and need no station integration.

**POC-1 — the separation histogram (does YOUR room separate?).**
1. Harvest ~10 frames per intended named view/entity from the dock's real 320×240 stream,
   spanning pan/tilt angles + daytime AND lamp-only night.
2. Embed each with DINOv2 ViT-S/14 (CLS token) — one Python script, no station code.
3. Plot two distance histograms: *same-entity* (different angle/time) vs
   *different-entity-same-room*. **Kill criterion:** if they overlap (no clean threshold),
   generic embeddings can't separate your entities off-the-shelf — stop, or budget fine-tuning.
   Break out R@1 by same-angle vs cross-angle and same-time vs cross-time to quantify the ceiling.

**POC-2 — the Metal timing probe (does it run cheap enough?).**
- Time DINOv2-S and (if things are in scope) OWL-ViT per-frame on the station's Metal GPU at
  the intended 1–4 Hz, alongside the existing vision+face load. **Kill criterion:** if OWL-ViT
  can't hit a usable rate without heavy quantization, "things" is deferred regardless of accuracy.

## Verdict (one paragraph)

Direction is right; the text-correlation half is already built and validated by the evidence.
The viable visual slice is **faces (built) + a handful of deliberately-distinct named views,
enrolled from multiple angles, corrected by the human as accumulating evidence** — NOT
within-room corner granularity (fails off-the-shelf), NOT reliable one-shot cross-view
correction (embeddings cluster by pose), NOT silent auto-naming (confidently wrong), NOT
store-everything (net-negative). Build nothing until POC-1's histogram on orbit's own frames
clears the bar.

## Update log

- 2026-07-08 — initial study. 10 research sub-agents (VPR, few-shot, open-vocab detection,
  VLM temporal/memory, semantic-SLAM, memory failure modes, correction generalization). All
  numbers are external benchmarks (ceilings). No POC run yet; POC-1/POC-2 are the next step.
